description: Silent .catch(() => {}) on all storage writes. Pattern appears 7 times in App.svelte. IndexedDB full, write fails mid-transaction, browser terminates connection — user sees nothing. Manual saves and batch rollback saves have no retry, no notification, no fallback.
resolution: Replace .catch(() => {}) with error propagation to toast/notification. Add retry for transient failures. Surface persistent failures in UI.

description: Auto-save marks saved even when IndexedDB write fails. autoSave store tracks consecutive failures and disables after 3, but .catch(() => {}) swallows error before store can track it. Status indicator shows saved while data is lost.
resolution: Propagate write errors to autoSave store. Show save-failed status with retry option. Don't mark saved until write confirmation.

description: No quota monitoring or threshold dialog trigger. storageUsage polled every 30s, displayed in StatusBar. storageFullDialog exists in dialogs store but nothing connects monitoring to dialog — no threshold check, no programmatic trigger.
resolution: Wire storageUsage polling to threshold check (90% quota). Trigger storageFullDialog when exceeded. Proactive warning at 80%.

description: loadProject() returns null on corrupt blob with no recovery UI. Returns null on gzip magic byte (legacy React format), malformed JSON from partial write, or string undefined. App starts empty — no diagnostic dialog, no recovery attempt.
resolution: Add recovery UI: detect corrupt/legacy blobs, show diagnostic dialog (retry, export raw, start fresh). Keep backup of last-known-good state.

description: No beforeunload handler to force save on tab close. Unsaved changes lost when user closes tab/browser. No prompt to save, no emergency flush to IndexedDB or localStorage.
resolution: Add beforeunload handler triggering synchronous save attempt. Use navigator.sendBeacon() as fallback. Warn user of unsaved changes.

description: IDB open race condition: lazy getDb() initialization without mutex. If multiple storage operations (saveProject, saveAsset) fire before first getDb() completes, multiple concurrent openDB() calls race. Only one connection cached; others may be dropped or create duplicate handles.
resolution: Add mutex/lock around getDb() initialization. Use a shared promise pattern: if init in progress, return existing promise instead of starting new open.

description: Auto-save resetFailures() logic error: sets saveStatus to 'saved' regardless of dirty flag. Code reads: this.#saveStatus = this.#dirty ? 'saved' : 'saved' — always 'saved'. After clearing quota errors, UI shows saved even if changes are pending.
resolution: Fix conditional: should be this.#saveStatus = this.#dirty ? 'error' : 'saved'. Or better: only set saved after confirmed write.

description: OPFS write durability: files written via createWritable()/write()/close() without fsync. FileSystemWritableFileStream.close() does not guarantee durability. Browser crash or power loss after return can produce truncated/corrupted files. No recovery mechanism for partial OPFS writes.
resolution: Call flush() before close(), or implement write-then-rename pattern. Add integrity check (size/hash) on read.

description: IDB connection never closed. Cached #db connection persists for entire app lifetime. Blocks other tabs from upgrading database schema (blocked() callback). On long sessions (hours), stale connection prevents version migration if user opens app in another tab.
resolution: Implement connection lifecycle: close on visibility change (hidden), reopen on focus. Or handle versionchange event to close and reopen.

name: Dual save path: immediate fire-and-forget plus debounced auto-save
description: handleUpdateRoot calls both autoSave.markDirty() and storage.saveProject().catch(() => {}) immediately. Creates two save paths: debounced auto-save effect AND immediate fire-and-forget. If immediate save fails silently, debounce timer may have already fired, no re-attempt. Double IDB writes on success.

JSON.stringify blocks main thread on large trees. saveProject() calls JSON.stringify(root) synchronously with no timeout or streaming. For 10K+ canvas manifests, serialization can block UI for hundreds of milliseconds. No Web Worker offloading or incremental serialization.
resolution: Move serialization to Web Worker or use streaming JSON serializer. At minimum, requestIdleCallback for non-critical saves.

description: No concurrent write coordinator. Multiple vault mutations can trigger saveProject from different UI paths (viewer annotation save, metadata batch edit, undo/redo). All call autoSave.markDirty() separately. If two fire within debounce window, only one snapshot saved. Intermediate mutations between snapshot and persist can be lost.
resolution: Implement write queue: serialize saves, ensure each takes latest vault state. Or use single save channel that always exports fresh state.

name: Four IndexedDB databases in architecture
description: Four separate IDB databases: (1) biiif-archive-db (v6, storage.ts): project state + original files. (2) field-studio-tiles (v1, tilePipeline.ts): pre-generated tile pyramids. (3) field-studio-discovery (changeDiscoveryService.ts): activity/change tracking log. (4) biiif-integrity-db (fileIntegrity.ts): file integrity checksums. No cross-database transactions or unified schema management.

name: OPFS storage service pre-built but unused
description: opfsStorage.ts contains complete OPFSStorage class with initialize(), storeFile(), getFile(), deleteFile(), and isSupported(). Never instantiated or called anywhere in codebase. Ready for activation when large-file storage needs OPFS fallback. Has 1s timeout race issue (recorded separately).

name: ChangeDiscovery as separate activity persistence
description: changeDiscoveryService.ts is a standalone activity tracking system in its own IDB database (field-studio-discovery). Logs user actions with timestamps for change discovery API export. Completely independent from project persistence (biiif-archive-db). Fire-and-forget writes with .catch(() => {}). No recovery on IDB failure — activity history silently incomplete.

name: Auto-save lockout with no UI notification
description: autoSave.svelte.ts: MAX_FAILURES=3, isDisabled becomes true, saves stop silently. No component reads isDisabled for user warning.

name: ViewerView annotation save has no error handling
description: ViewerView.handleCreateAnnotation calls storage.saveProject(updatedRoot) with no await, no try/catch, no .catch(). IDB failure silently loses the annotation — user sees it in UI but it's not persisted.
